title: Assign and correct transaction categories
description: Maintain one attributable active category allocation per active transaction without rewriting transaction source facts. Implements G3 and UC-LEDGER-003 under A5.
rationale: Explicit, correctable allocation is the canonical input to BUDGET and INSIGHTS while classification remains a separate concern.
story_want: assign and correct the category of a transaction
story_so_that: spend across accounts uses consistent categories
acceptance_criteria: Given an active transaction and active category, when the owner assigns the category, then exactly one active allocation is returned and the transaction source facts remain unchanged.

Given a categorized active transaction, an active replacement category, and a reason, when the owner corrects the allocation, then the prior allocation is retired, the replacement becomes active, and both remain attributable in history.
Given an inactive transaction or archived category, when an assignment or correction is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no allocation.
Given a category rename, when historical and active assignments are queried, then they retain the same category identifier and resolve to the current display name.

Given a Category Assignment or correction, when it is applied, then account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, evidence, and Reconciliation State remain unchanged unless changed through their own explicit operations.
